jokes.text: Why would you be inviting Louie? / You just tell him what you told us... and duck.
jokes.setup: Zena wants to invite Louie to her wedding despite their past relationship
jokes.punchline: Alex's practical advice to 'duck' after telling Louie
jokes.explanation: Good character moment for Alex being protective, nice economy in the punchline

jokes.text: The boss's girlfriend. / Zena's getting married! / Oh, congratulations! That's great. See that? Why... that boss. The monkey never said a word.
jokes.setup: Jim assumes Zena is marrying Louie and congratulates her
jokes.punchline: Jim calls Louie a 'monkey' for not telling them about the engagement
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Jim's loyalty to Louie, calling him 'monkey' is endearing

jokes.text: Another man? Oh, perfidy! Thy name is woman!
jokes.setup: Jim discovers Zena is marrying someone other than Louie
jokes.punchline: Jim's overly dramatic, pseudo-Shakespearean response
jokes.explanation: Perfect character integration - only Jim would respond this way with pseudo-literary language

jokes.text: I caught one back there. He didn't even finish the cheese.
jokes.setup: Iggy randomly appears talking about catching something
jokes.punchline: He's talking about a rat trap, completely oblivious to the conversation
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Iggy's disconnect from social situations

jokes.text: You're looking good. / Oh, thanks. I got a mirror that tells me that.
jokes.setup: Louie compliments Zena's appearance
jokes.punchline: Zena's deflecting response about her mirror
jokes.explanation: Decent comeback line, shows Zena's discomfort with Louie's compliment

jokes.text: What's on my calendar? / A roach.
jokes.setup: Louie asks Jeff to check his calendar
jokes.punchline: Jeff deadpan reports there's literally a roach on it
jokes.explanation: Excellent economy and structure - perfect subversion of expectation with visual absurdity

jokes.text: Oh, oui, oui.
jokes.setup: Zena mentions meeting at a French restaurant
jokes.punchline: Louie's attempt at French sophistication
jokes.explanation: Predictable language joke, though it fits Louie's character trying to be suave

jokes.text: Operation Crawlback.
jokes.setup: Louie sees Zena asking him out
jokes.punchline: His military-style code name for getting back together
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Louie's ego and military-style thinking

jokes.text: I got to go home and change my sheets.
jokes.setup: Louie thinks Zena wants to get back together
jokes.punchline: His presumptuous preparation for romance
jokes.explanation: Perfect Louie moment - crude presumption that makes audience cringe

jokes.text: I missed her, too.
jokes.setup: Alex challenges Louie's bravado about Zena
jokes.punchline: Louie's rare moment of vulnerability
jokes.explanation: Excellent character moment - shows Louie's humanity beneath the bluster

jokes.text: Nope. Nothing's changed?
jokes.setup: Zena asks if there have been changes in Louie's life, clearly fishing for information about other women
jokes.punchline: Louie gives a flat 'Nope' indicating his life is exactly as pathetic as when she left
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan delivery that reveals Louie's stagnant existence. Maximum impact with minimum words.

jokes.text: There have been others, and as good as they were, none of 'em earned the Golden Louie.
jokes.setup: Louie talks about other relationships
jokes.punchline: His self-aggrandizing award system
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character-specific joke - only Louie would create his own award system

jokes.text: The door is open, Zena. You've suffered enough. Come in out of the rain.
jokes.setup: Louie thinks he's being magnanimous
jokes.punchline: His condescending 'forgiveness' of Zena
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Louie's delusional self-importance

jokes.text: You know, I always thought that I would forgive you, but not this quick.
jokes.setup: Zena announces her engagement
jokes.punchline: Louie's misinterpretation - he thinks SHE'S asking for forgiveness
jokes.explanation: Excellent character comedy - shows Louie's complete inability to grasp reality

jokes.text: Happy for you? Oh... Yeah, I'm happy for you. I'm happy. Yeah, I'm-I'm happy for you. We're happy for her, right? Yeah, yeah, that's good. We're happy for you. We're happy. We're happy. We're all happy. Whee! We're happy for you.
jokes.setup: Zena asks if Louie can be happy for her
jokes.punchline: His increasingly manic repetition of 'happy' ending with 'Whee!'
jokes.explanation: Masterful escalation showing Louie's breakdown, the 'Whee!' is perfectly absurd

jokes.text: Who's the next victim?
jokes.setup: Louie asks who Zena is marrying
jokes.punchline: He refers to her fiancé as a 'victim'
jokes.explanation: Good dark humor showing Louie's bitter perspective on Zena's relationships

jokes.text: His name is Tom Pelton. He's a child psychiatrist, and we met six months... / That's enough.
jokes.setup: Zena starts to explain about her fiancé
jokes.punchline: Louie cuts her off immediately - he can't bear to hear details
jokes.explanation: Good timing and character truth - Louie can't handle the reality

jokes.text: Would you at least have the decency to go to bed with me for one last time?
jokes.setup: Louie claims he won't make things difficult
jokes.punchline: Immediately asks for one last sexual encounter
jokes.explanation: Perfect Louie moment - shows his inability to be decent even when trying

jokes.text: Well, then I'll repeat it. Would you at least have the decency...?
jokes.setup: Zena says she'll forget he said that
jokes.punchline: Louie immediately starts to repeat the inappropriate request
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing Louie's complete lack of shame or social awareness

jokes.text: She didn't even look back!
jokes.setup: Zena leaves after his inappropriate behavior
jokes.punchline: Louie is shocked she didn't look back at him
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Louie's wounded ego and inability to understand consequences

jokes.text: You know, I'm not gonna pay for a damn thing on this table. / I didn't say I wasn't gonna eat it. I'm just not gonna pay for it.
jokes.setup: Louie declares he won't pay for dinner
jokes.punchline: Clarifies he'll still eat everything, just not pay
jokes.explanation: Perfect Louie logic - petty revenge but still getting his money's worth

jokes.text: What the hell are you looking at, Mac? Huh? You never seen a man eat French food before, huh?
jokes.setup: Louie eating alone at the restaurant
jokes.punchline: He confronts other diners for looking at him
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of Louie's public breakdown, shows his defensiveness

jokes.text: How we doing, Jeff? / You got eyes.
jokes.setup: Alex asks about Jeff's condition
jokes.punchline: Jeff's terse response implying Alex can see for himself how bad it is
jokes.explanation: Excellent economy and deadpan delivery, shows Jeff's misery clearly

jokes.text: I can't believe I caught the bouquet!
jokes.setup: The group returns from Zena's wedding
jokes.punchline: Jim, a man, caught the bridal bouquet
jokes.explanation: Good visual comedy and character moment for Jim's enthusiasm

jokes.text: Was that today? Oh, for crying out loud. Hey, what the heck? I can't be runnin' off to weddings every day havin' a good time.
jokes.setup: Louie pretends he forgot about the wedding
jokes.punchline: His excuse that he can't be 'running off to weddings every day'
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Louie's defensive pride and fake indifference

jokes.text: He was telling me great things about being a child psychiatrist, and I was telling him great things about being a neighborhood guy.
jokes.setup: Tony describes talking to Zena's new husband
jokes.punchline: The contrast between 'child psychiatrist' and 'neighborhood guy'
jokes.explanation: Good character moment for Tony's self-awareness about his humble status

jokes.text: We made bets on who would see the most disgusting things floating around in the water. I let her win.
jokes.setup: Louie reminisces about taking Zena on a boat ride
jokes.punchline: Their 'romantic' activity was betting on sewage, and he 'let her win'
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character moment - only Louie would think this was romantic, and his 'chivalry' is letting her win at spotting sewage

jokes.text: And now she's gonna be out there on that deck looking for raw sewage with another guy.
jokes.setup: Continuation of the sewage-spotting memory
jokes.punchline: Louie's genuine heartbreak that she'll share 'their' activity with her new husband
jokes.explanation: Masterful callback that deepens the absurdity while showing genuine emotion - peak Louie

jokes.text: Your last chance to prove that you're a decent human being and leave Zena with a... with a loving memory of Louie De Palma. And you blew it!
jokes.setup: Alex confronts Louie about missing the wedding
jokes.punchline: The phrase 'loving memory of Louie De Palma' is inherently contradictory given Louie's nature
jokes.explanation: Good character moment for Alex as the moral center, with the inherent irony that a 'loving memory of Louie De Palma' might be impossible.

jokes.text: Happiness is hard to come by in this life, and you've given me more than my share.
jokes.setup: Alex explains what Louie could have said to Zena
jokes.punchline: His beautiful, heartfelt suggestion
jokes.explanation: Beautiful writing that becomes important later, shows Alex's wisdom and heart

jokes.text: What a way to go out! What a man I'd be to say that.
jokes.setup: Louie repeats Alex's beautiful sentiment
jokes.punchline: His admiration for how classy he would sound saying it
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Louie's self-awareness and desire to be better

jokes.text: Friendship is a gift of fate, and I thank the gods for sending you. People who just say 'hi' in this life don't know what they're missing.
jokes.setup: Louie is inspired to add more beautiful sentiments
jokes.punchline: He keeps improvising increasingly flowery language
jokes.explanation: Shows Louie getting carried away with his newfound eloquence, less impactful than the original

jokes.text: Uh, well, um, there's, um, bingo at 7:30. Uh-huh. And then, uh, there's a movie, The Poseidon Adventure at 8:00.
jokes.setup: Newlyweds on their honeymoon cruise discussing plans
jokes.punchline: The mundane, elderly activities available on their 'romantic' cruise
jokes.explanation: Mild observational humor about cruise ship activities being unglamorous

jokes.text: Don't mind me. I know how it is with young marrieds.
jokes.setup: Louie interrupts the newlyweds' honeymoon
jokes.punchline: His inappropriate comment about their sexuality
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment showing Louie's complete lack of boundaries

jokes.text: It's kinda like meetin' a legend, isn't it, Tom? Ah, I understand how difficult it must be competing with the ghost of Louie De Palma.
jokes.setup: Louie introduces himself to Tom
jokes.punchline: His grandiose self-perception as a 'legend' and a 'ghost'
jokes.explanation: Excellent character moment showing Louie's delusional self-importance

jokes.text: It's kinda like meetin' a legend, isn't it, Tom?
jokes.setup: Louie introduces himself to Tom
jokes.punchline: He refers to himself as a 'legend' that Tom should feel honored to meet
jokes.explanation: Peak Louie narcissism - positioning himself as legendary while crashing their honeymoon. The casual delivery makes it funnier.

jokes.text: You mean that this is the first time my name ever came up? Oh, I get it. Oh, that's very decent of you, Zena. To spare old Tom here the pain of the inevitable comparison.
jokes.setup: Tom seems unaware of Louie's significance
jokes.punchline: Louie's wounded ego interpretation that Zena was 'sparing' Tom
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of Louie's delusion and wounded pride

jokes.text: Well, what'd you think, you beat out the governor and the president to get her? This is what you beat out. Right here, look, this is it. It was you or this.
jokes.setup: Tom expresses disbelief about Louie dating Zena
jokes.punchline: Louie's bitter self-aware putdown of himself
jokes.explanation: Excellent self-aware moment that's both funny and poignant - Louie knows exactly what he is

jokes.text: Well, what'd you think, you beat out the governor and the president to get her? This is what you beat out. Right here, look, this is it. It was you or this.
jokes.setup: Tom seems confused about who Louie is
jokes.punchline: Louie points to himself as Tom's 'competition,' ironically highlighting his own unimpressive nature
jokes.explanation: Brilliant self-aware moment where Louie inadvertently highlights his own mediocrity while trying to make a point. The physical gesture adds to the comedy.

jokes.text: Honey, I'm a psychiatrist. It's my job. I'm trained to understand deviant behavior.
jokes.setup: Tom claims to understand Louie
jokes.punchline: He casually refers to Louie as 'deviant behavior'
jokes.explanation: Excellent putdown delivered with clinical detachment - very effective

jokes.text: How 'bout this? Like that? You're a psychiatrist. Why am I doing this, huh? Tell me, Doc.
jokes.setup: Tom tries to psychoanalyze Louie's behavior
jokes.punchline: Louie starts physically attacking Tom while asking for psychological analysis of his own violent behavior
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation where Louie simultaneously acts out and requests analysis. The juxtaposition of violence with intellectual curiosity is perfectly absurd.

jokes.text: I'm doing it because I hate your guts. And because it makes me feel good.
jokes.setup: Tom asks why Louie is attacking him
jokes.punchline: Louie's brutally honest, simple explanation
jokes.explanation: Perfect Louie moment - refreshingly honest about his motivations

jokes.text: And to think, I ran all the way over here just to show a little class. And what do I do? I wind up strangling your husband and stealing his watch.
jokes.setup: Louie reflects on his behavior
jokes.punchline: His casual mention of strangling and theft as if they're minor social faux pas
jokes.explanation: Excellent character moment showing Louie's skewed perspective on what constitutes bad behavior

jokes.text: Happiness is hard to come by in this life... and you've given me more than my share.
jokes.setup: Louie finally delivers Alex's beautiful line
jokes.punchline: The genuine emotion behind the words
jokes.explanation: Masterful callback that delivers the emotional payoff - becomes beautiful when Louie means it

jokes.text: It's not my line. That was Reiger's line.
jokes.setup: Zena is moved by Louie's beautiful words
jokes.punchline: Louie immediately credits Alex, showing his honesty
jokes.explanation: Beautiful character moment showing Louie's fundamental honesty even in his moment of glory

jokes.text: I'm never gonna do better than you. You were the best woman that I ever had... and I let you go.
jokes.setup: Louie wants to share his real feelings
jokes.punchline: His raw, honest admission of loss and regret
jokes.explanation: Peak emotional moment for Louie - completely honest and vulnerable, earns the sentiment

jokes.text: When you see repulsive things floating around in the water... I'll be thinking of you.
jokes.setup: Louie's final goodbye to Zena
jokes.punchline: He references their sewage-spotting romantic memory
jokes.explanation: Perfect ending - transforms their disgusting shared memory into something genuinely touching through Louie's sincere delivery
